name: When should I hire someone or use a specialist tool to handle AI share of voice tracking?
description: Once you go beyond a small manual pilot and AI share of voice starts informing real content and authority decisions, assign a dedicated owner or use specialized

mainEntity.name: When should I hire someone or use a specialist tool to handle AI share of voice tracking?

acceptedAnswer.text:
For most teams, it’s smart to bring in a specialist or dedicated owner once your AI share of voice program moves beyond a small manual pilot. Complexity grows quickly as you add prompts, engines, and nuance like positioning and citations.

Context: Early on, a marketer or analyst can manage a lightweight spreadsheet. At scale, you need someone responsible for methodology, tooling, and translating findings into content and authority actions.

Key factors:
- Number of prompts and AI engines in scope
- Need for consistent, defensible reporting
- Integration with SEO, content, and PR workflows
- Strategic importance of AI visibility to the business

Practical guidance: Start small, but define who will own AI SOV before it becomes "everyone’s part-time project." When decisions or budgets hinge on these metrics, having a specialist or partner is worth the investment.

Soft positioning: Treat the decision to hire help as a signal that AI share of voice has become a core, not experimental, metric.
acceptedAnswer.description: Once you go beyond a small manual pilot and AI share of voice starts informing real content and authority decisions, assign a dedicated owner or use specialized tools. Complexity and the need for consistent, defensible reporting grow quickly.
